damage_types.name: Flood Damage
immediate_steps: Ensure your safety first — do not enter flooded fields
Take clear photos/videos of all damaged crop areas
Report damage to nearest Agriculture Office within 72 hours
Inform your bank if you have KCC or crop loan
File insurance claim if enrolled in PMFBY
Do not remove or replant until inspection is done

compensation.pmfby: Full claim for flooded/waterlogged crops under PMFBY
compensation.seed_replacement: Free seeds/fertilizers for next season from Agriculture Department

claim_process: Report to insurance company/bank within 72 hours
Submit claim form with required documents at bank branch or CSC
Insurance company conducts field inspection / crop cutting experiment
Claim amount is credited directly to your bank account
Contact insurance officer if payment is delayed beyond 30 days
post_recovery_tips: Drain excess water from fields as soon as possible
Apply gypsum @ 500 kg/hectare to restore soil structure
Zinc deficiency is common after floods — apply ZnSO₄ 25kg/hectare
Replant short-duration varieties if possible
Monitor surviving crop for disease outbreaks
Apply fungicide preventively on standing crop

damage_types.name: Drought Damage
immediate_steps: Document crop loss with clear photos and videos
Report to local Agriculture Department office
File insurance claim within 72 hours if enrolled in PMFBY
Conserve remaining water for the most critical crops
Apply mulching to reduce evaporation
Contact District Agriculture Officer for relief information

compensation.pmfby: PMFBY covers drought — file claim immediately if insured
compensation.state_relief: State government announces drought relief package including input subsidy
compensation.other: Free fodder for livestock, MGNREGA 100-day work guarantee

claim_process: Visit nearest bank or CSC with damage photos
Submit insurance claim form within stipulated time
Agriculture officer conducts joint inspection
Claims processed and credited to bank account
Apply for input subsidy at Agriculture Office if not insured
post_recovery_tips: Apply mulching (straw/plastic) to retain soil moisture
Use potassium-rich fertilizers to help plants withstand stress
Consider short-duration drought-tolerant crops for next season (Bajra, Moong, Groundnut)
Plan rainwater harvesting structures — farm pond, check dam
Use drip irrigation if available — saves 30-50% water
Practice zero-tillage to maintain soil moisture

damage_types.name: Cyclone Damage
immediate_steps: Prioritize personal safety — do not enter fields during cyclone
After cyclone passes, assess and document all damage
Take photos of flattened crops, broken trees, damaged infrastructure
Report within 72 hours to Agriculture Department and Insurance company
Contact nearest CSC for insurance claim assistance

compensation.pmfby: Full claim under PMFBY for cyclone-damaged crops
compensation.input_subsidy: State government announces special input subsidy for cyclone affected farmers
compensation.infrastructure: Compensation for damaged farm infrastructure (pumps, shed, fencing)

claim_process: File claim with insurance company through bank or CSC
Submit claim with all required documents within 72 hours
Get village officer to verify crop loss assessment
Insurance company processes claim
Funds credited to bank account within 15-30 days
post_recovery_tips: Clear debris from fields carefully
Remove damaged plant material to prevent disease
Apply balanced fertilizer to help surviving crops recover
Stake/trellis damaged fruit plants if possible
Apply fungicide as preventive measure on surviving plants
Re-sow short-duration crops for remaining season

damage_types.name: Hailstorm Damage
immediate_steps: Take clear photos of all damage within 48 hours
Report hailstorm damage to local Agriculture Department immediately
File insurance claim if crop is insured under PMFBY
Contact your bank or nearest CSC for claim assistance

compensation.pmfby: Full compensation if crop loss exceeds 50%
compensation.sdrf_subsidy: Input subsidy of ₹13,500/hectare (max 2 hectares) from SDRF
compensation.seeds: Free seed distribution for re-sowing from Agriculture Department

claim_process: Report to bank/CSC within 48 hours of hailstorm event
Submit claim with damage photos and documents
Junta/Patwari conducts field inspection
Claim approved and amount credited to bank account
post_recovery_tips: Prune damaged branches in orchards to encourage fresh growth
Apply Copper-based fungicide to prevent infection on damaged plant parts
Spray NPK 19:19:19 @ 5g/L to help plants recover
For severely damaged crops, re-sow immediately if season permits
Remove and destroy completely damaged plant material

damage_types.name: Pest Attack / Locust
immediate_steps: Correctly identify the pest before taking action
Report pest outbreak to nearest Agriculture Department immediately
Inform neighboring farmers to take preventive measures together
Do not use broad-spectrum pesticides without proper identification
Contact Agriculture Officer for recommended pesticides and dosage
required_documents: Photos of pest infestation on crop

compensation.pmfby: PMFBY covers pest/disease attacks — file claim if enrolled
compensation.state_assistance: Some states provide free pesticides through government supply
compensation.preventive_support: Preventive aerial spraying in locust-affected areas by government

claim_process: Report pest attack to nearest Agriculture Office
Get pest identification report from Agriculture Officer
Apply recommended control measures immediately
File insurance claim for crop damage within time limit
Submit photos and officer's report with insurance claim
post_recovery_tips: Practice crop rotation to break pest life cycles
Use pheromone traps and light traps for pest monitoring
Grow pest-resistant varieties in next season
Maintain natural enemies — avoid unnecessary pesticide use
Plant barrier crops like maize around main field
Deep summer ploughing destroys pupae in soil
